name: Increase PTX Grouped-Row Depth On The Clean Baseline

summary.name: Increase PTX Grouped-Row Depth On The Clean Baseline
summary.hypothesis: The accepted PTX hot-band baseline may still be wasting issue slots because each hot-band B tile is only reused across four adjacent row tiles before the traversal advances columns. Increasing kFixedHotBandPtxGroupedRows from 4 to 8 should keep each B tile live across more rows, potentially reducing scoreboard pressure without changing the row split or K-stage structure.
summary.expected_bottleneck: launch-order and B-tile reuse inefficiency inside the accepted PTX hot-band traversal
summary.risk: Low-to-moderate risk: the edit is isolated, but deeper row grouping can help B reuse while hurting A locality or cache balance.
ranking_notes: This is the fastest clean experiment to run on the accepted PTX hot-band baseline.
It keeps the search on the same family that currently produces the best custom runtime.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if runtime change stays within noise or if long-scoreboard stalls rise after the first clean measurement.
expected_local_changes: Keep the accepted PTX hot-band row split and default launch intact.
Increase kFixedHotBandPtxGroupedRows from 4 to 8 and remeasure on the same fixed benchmark.

notes: Node C may now implement this one candidate.
